game_name: World of Illusion Starring Mickey Mouse and Donald Duck

design_philosophy.core_loop: Platform traversal with cape-based combat and spell acquisition. Players progress through themed levels, defeat bosses to learn new magic spells that unlock subsequent levels.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Route variation based on character selection (Mickey vs Donald) and cooperative play mechanics. Different character abilities create divergent level design.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience Disney animation as an interactive adventure. Navigate magical worlds inspired by classic Disney films while using magical abilities (flying carpets, underwater bubbles) to progress.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Character-driven level design philosophy—Mickey and Donald experience fundamentally different routes through the same stages due to distinct abilities (Mickey squeezes through gaps; Donald finds alternative paths). Cooperative play emphasizes collaboration over competition. Strong thematic adherence to Disney animated film aesthetics and narrative framing.

difficulty.lives_system: Health pool represented by face-down cards; damage reduces card count
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with boss encounters marking stage completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Single-player vs two-player modes affect challenge scaling. Character selection (Mickey vs Donald) creates different difficulty curves due to route variations and ability differences. Two-player mode shares lives pool, affecting resource management.

progression.upgrade_system: Magic spell acquisition through boss defeats. Each boss grants new spell enabling access to next level (flying on magic carpet for cloud level; air bubble for underwater level).
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss progression gates. Spells learned from bosses unlock thematic traversal abilities for subsequent stages.
replayability_hooks: Character selection creates distinct level routes and experiences
Two-player cooperative mode vs single-player offers different gameplay dynamics
Optional sub-stages accessible only in specific play modes
Divergent pathways within shared stages based on character abilities

world_building_techniques: Thematic level design (Enchanted Forest, Cloud Kingdom, Underwater, Library, Magic Box)
Direct visual references to Disney animated films (Alice in Wonderland card soldiers, Snow White, Pinocchio, Fantasia, Sleeping Beauty, The Sword in the Stone, The Little Mermaid influences)
Magical transformation mechanics reinforcing narrative progression (defeated enemies become doves, cards, harmless objects)

boss_design.philosophy: Story-driven ability gatekeepers. Each boss defeat grants thematic magical spell that enables traversal of next stage. Design integrates boss powers into world navigation.

danger_signals: Evil magician's control over dimensional barriers
environmental_storytelling: Themed worlds reflect magical domain control (clouds, underwater, library suggest progressive invasion of reality)
Card soldiers and Disney character references establish magical precedent
Spell acquisition visualized through boss encounters
Transformation effects (enemies becoming harmless doves) suggest magical neutralization philosophy
